sources.title: RFC 7231 HTTP Semantics and Content
sources.fallback_text: RFC-7231 is titled Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP/1.1): Semantics and Content. It defines HTTP methods, status code semantics, request semantics, and response semantics.

sources.title: RFC 9110 HTTP Semantics
sources.fallback_text: RFC-9110 is titled HTTP Semantics. It revises and consolidates HTTP semantic definitions.

sources.title: RFC 7235 HTTP Authentication
sources.fallback_text: RFC-7235 defines Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP/1.1): Authentication. It describes HTTP authentication framework behavior and challenges.

sources.title: RFC 3986 URI Generic Syntax
sources.fallback_text: RFC-3986 defines Uniform Resource Identifier (URI): Generic Syntax. It covers URI syntax and components.

sources.title: 45 CFR 164.512 Uses and Disclosures
sources.expected_answer: uses and disclosures for which authorization or opportunity to agree or object is not required
sources.fallback_text: 45 CFR 164.512 covers uses and disclosures for which an authorization or opportunity to agree or object is not required. It is distinct from 164.510 and 164.514.

sources.title: 45 CFR 164.510 Uses Requiring Opportunity To Agree Or Object
sources.expected_answer: uses and disclosures requiring an opportunity to agree or object
sources.fallback_text: 45 CFR 164.510 covers uses and disclosures requiring an opportunity for the individual to agree or object.

sources.title: ICD-10 J18.9 Pneumonia Unspecified Organism
sources.fallback_text: ICD-10-CM code J18.9 identifies pneumonia, unspecified organism. Nearby codes include J18.0 and J18.1.

sources.title: PyTorch torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss
sources.expected_answer: criterion that computes cross entropy loss
sources.fallback_text: torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss is a PyTorch criterion that computes cross entropy loss between logits and targets. It is different from torch.nn.NLLLoss and torch.nn.BCELoss.

sources.title: NumPy numpy.linalg.svd
sources.fallback_text: numpy.linalg.svd computes the singular value decomposition of an array.

sources.title: PostgreSQL JSONB
sources.fallback_text: PostgreSQL jsonb stores JSON data in a decomposed binary format that supports indexing and efficient querying.

sources.title: Python dataclasses
sources.expected_answer: decorator and functions for automatically adding generated special methods
sources.fallback_text: Python dataclasses provide a decorator and functions for automatically adding generated special methods to classes.

sources.title: scikit-learn TfidfVectorizer
sources.expected_answer: convert raw documents to a matrix of TF-IDF features
sources.fallback_text: sklearn.feature_extraction.text.TfidfVectorizer converts raw documents to a matrix of TF-IDF features.

sources.title: Climate Anxiety
sources.fallback_text: Climate anxiety describes distress, worry, fear, or grief connected to climate change and environmental loss.

sources.title: Photosynthesis
sources.fallback_text: Photosynthesis is the biological process by which plants, algae, and some bacteria convert light energy into chemical energy.

sources.title: Urban Heat Island
sources.fallback_text: An urban heat island is an urban area that is significantly warmer than nearby rural areas because surfaces store heat.

sources.title: Memory Consolidation
sources.fallback_text: Memory consolidation is the process by which a temporary memory becomes more stable and durable over time.

sources.title: Algorithmic Bias
sources.fallback_text: Algorithmic bias describes systematic and repeatable errors in computer systems that create unfair outcomes.

sources.title: Circadian Rhythm
sources.fallback_text: A circadian rhythm is an internal biological process that follows a roughly 24-hour cycle.

sources.title: Reinforcement Learning
sources.fallback_text: Reinforcement learning is an area of machine learning where an agent learns behavior from rewards and penalties.

sources.title: Circular Economy
sources.fallback_text: A circular economy aims to keep products and materials in use and reduce waste through reuse and recycling.

sources.title: Water Cycle
sources.fallback_text: The water cycle describes the continuous movement of water through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and runoff.

sources.title: Allergy
sources.fallback_text: An allergy is an immune system reaction to a foreign substance that is usually harmless to most people.

sources.title: Diffusion of Innovations
sources.fallback_text: Diffusion of innovations explains how new ideas, technologies, or practices spread through a social system.

sources.title: Distributional Semantics
sources.fallback_text: Distributional semantics represents meaning from the contexts in which words appear.

sources.title: Bat
sources.fallback_text: A bat is a mammal. Bats are vertebrates and are capable of powered flight using wings and echolocation.

sources.title: Penguin
sources.fallback_text: A penguin is a bird. Penguins are vertebrates and are not capable of flight but are capable of swimming.

sources.title: Dolphin
sources.fallback_text: A dolphin is a mammal. Dolphins are vertebrates that produce milk and are capable of swimming.

sources.title: Whale
sources.fallback_text: A whale is a mammal. Whales are vertebrates that produce milk and are capable of swimming.

sources.title: Salmon
sources.fallback_text: A salmon is a fish. Salmon are vertebrates and are capable of swimming.

sources.title: Mosquito
sources.fallback_text: A mosquito is an insect. Mosquitoes have wings and are capable of flying.

sources.title: Ostrich
sources.fallback_text: An ostrich is a bird. Ostriches are vertebrates and are not capable of flight.

sources.title: Eagle
sources.fallback_text: An eagle is a bird. Eagles are vertebrates and are capable of flying.

sources.title: Frog
sources.fallback_text: A frog is an amphibian and a vertebrate. Frogs can swim and jump.

sources.title: Mammal
sources.fallback_text: A mammal is a vertebrate animal. Mammals produce milk and include bats, dolphins, whales, cats, and dogs.

sources.title: Bird
sources.fallback_text: A bird is a warm-blooded vertebrate animal. Birds include penguins, eagles, owls, ducks, and sparrows.

sources.title: Vertebrate
sources.fallback_text: A vertebrate is an animal with a backbone or spinal column. Mammals, birds, fish, amphibians, and reptiles are vertebrates.

sources.title: Bat Echolocation
sources.fallback_text: Bats use echolocation and wings. A bat connects to vertebrate through mammal, and echolocation helps bats navigate.

sources.title: Penguin Swimming
sources.fallback_text: Penguins are birds and vertebrates. Their flippers and streamlined bodies connect penguins to swimming rather than flight.

sources.title: Dolphin Mammal Swimming
sources.fallback_text: A dolphin connects to vertebrate through mammal. Text evidence also describes dolphins as aquatic mammals capable of swimming.

sources.title: Salmon Fish Migration
sources.fallback_text: Salmon connect to vertebrate through fish. Public text describes salmon migration and swimming in river and ocean habitats.

sources.title: Mosquito Insect Flight
sources.fallback_text: A mosquito connects to animal through insect. Text evidence describes mosquito wings and flight.

sources.title: Ostrich Bird Counterexample
sources.fallback_text: An ostrich connects to vertebrate through bird, but it is a flightless bird and a counterexample for all birds fly.

sources.title: Eagle Bird Flight
sources.fallback_text: An eagle connects to vertebrate through bird. Text evidence describes eagle wings and flight.

sources.title: Whale Mammal Milk
sources.fallback_text: A whale connects to vertebrate through mammal. Text evidence describes whales as mammals that produce milk.

sources.title: Frog Amphibian Vertebrate
sources.fallback_text: A frog connects to animal through vertebrate. Text evidence describes frogs as amphibians with backbones.

sources.title: Duck Bird Swimming
sources.fallback_text: A duck connects to vertebrate through bird. Text evidence describes ducks as birds that can swim.

sources.title: Cat Mammal
sources.fallback_text: A cat connects to vertebrate through mammal. Text evidence describes cats as small carnivorous mammals.

sources.title: Owl Bird
sources.fallback_text: An owl connects to vertebrate through bird. Text evidence describes owls as birds of prey with wings.
